summary: Book Trilogy Atlas is a manuscript-control atlas for a governed three-book inquiry into power, democratic formation, violence, memory, and repair.
final_use_case: It matters because the trilogy's distinctive claim is architectural: power, democratic speech, and political repair have to be managed as one book system without flattening the three books into one voice or confusing public book order with canonical chapter IDs.

instruments.input: 72 canonical chapter IDs, public book order, book-title logic, and chapter-title state
instruments.operation: keeps the three-book system stable while public titles and presentation language are improved
instruments.output: chapter identity ledger and public/canonical mapping
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires 72 canonical chapter IDs, public book order, book-title logic, and chapter-title state
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds chapter identity ledger and public/canonical mapping; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: chapter identity ledger and public/canonical mapping
instruments.status: Status is deliberately conservative. The chapter map, governance scaffolds, source traces, and rewrite dossiers exist as public-representable evidence of the.
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept 72 canonical chapter IDs, public book order, book-title logic, and chapter-title state and produce chapter identity ledger and public/canonical mapping.
instruments.verification_check: Apply Canonical Chapter Identity to one chapter dossier; confirm title, visual, source state, rewrite target, and handoff decision.
